title: Live collaboration ships in three milestones: polling MVP, then GSOC real-time, then document-level scope
content:
## Build order: three milestones

### Milestone 1 — MVP (polling, no GSOC)

Goal: two users, one drive, invitation → accept → B's edit appears in A's toolbar History within ~5 s (existing poll interval). Proves the full loop on real Bee nodes.

Includes: types + manifest format, `CollabManager` (create/accept/list/leave), SwarmChannel peer-feed registration with per-source cursors, collab-scoped push path (second ACT upload), Chat UI Collaborate tab, `CollabInviteCard`, toolbar History auto-refresh.

### Milestone 2 — GSOC real-time upgrade

1. `GsocNotifier.onOpCommitted` — subscribe to each participant's signer.
2. Send pings on push — `op-committed` GSOC message to each other participant after a collab-feed update.
3. Debounced pull on ping — SwarmChannel pulls that writer's feed, coalescing bursts with ~300 ms debounce.
4. Background signer mining — first collab create/accept kicks off mining without blocking the UI; polling is the fallback until mining completes.
5. Latency smoke test: peer sees new revision in History within ~1–2 s of sender commit.

### Milestone 3 — Document-level scope

1. Narrow the ACT chain to the doc's feeds only (exclude drive manifest + sibling docs).
2. Participant join gets just the one doc, not the containing drive.
3. UI differentiates "Shared document: X" from drive-level collabs.
description: Three milestones: M1 polling MVP end-to-end, M2 adds GSOC sub-second real-time, M3 narrows scope to document-level collaboration.
